batches.notes: Sequential rows 2-21 (2-B Energy through AESC Spain). Sources: BusinessWire, GlobeNewsWire, Windpower Monthly, Automation.com, CompositesWorld, ACCIONA pressroom, RenewablesNow, PV-Magazine, Japan Times, La Moncloa. Flags: Aeolon row 17 cites Nador not Tanger; AESC Douai row 18 investment discrepancy ($920M vs ~€2B); AESC Sunderland row 20 investment discrepancy ($622M vs £1B). AAER row 5 Bordeaux/Valorem not independently confirmed — fallback fDi Markets (FT).

Sequential rows 102-121 (VW Hefei R&D through Toshiba Houston). Sources: volkswagen-group.com, govt.chinadaily.com.cn, pv-magazine.com, ess-news.com, gotion.com.cn, koreaherald.com, kedglobal.com, news.metal.com (Sinoma), prnewswire.com (Boston-Power), northvolt.com/scania, cbc.ca (Bolloré), farasis-energy.com, hipa.hu (KunlunChem), yicaiglobal.com (Pret), nec.com, valmet-automotive.com, ledinside.com, argusmedia.com, umicore.com, umicore.kr. Fallback fDi Markets (FT) for R120 (JinYang Malaysia — no Tier A/B source found after company IR + IPA + local-language passes) and R121 (Toshiba Houston — no public announcement found for $85M 2025 plant). A-O reconciliations: R105 G Hanoi→Ha Tinh (Gotion press release confirms Ha Tinh/Vung Ang; S flagged [UNVERIFIED] — $140M is approx Gotion 51% share of $275M total); R112 G Debrecen→Szolnok (HIPA confirms KunlunChem facility is in Szolnok, not Debrecen); R113 G Kuala Lumpur→Johor (batteriesnews confirms Johor state for Pret/Highstar factory); R115 I None→2023 (Valmet Automotive Kirchardt series production started Feb 2023); R117 G Jakarta→Kendal (Kendal SEZ Central Java, confirmed by Kendal Industrial Park and Argus Media); R118 M 0→1 / N None→1 / O None→Jiangmen Changxin Technology (Umicore groundbreaking press release confirms JUC is a JV with Jiangmen Changxin Technology). S column [UNVERIFIED] applied to R102, R104 (Batam city not confirmed by sources — Weda Bay suggested), R108, R109, R111, R114, R115, R116, R117, R118, R119 where amount was not unambiguously confirmed.

Sequential rows 122-141 (Kedali Malaysia through Johnson Controls Guardamar). Sources: yicaiglobal.com, theedgemalaysia.com, indiaesa.info, pv-tech.org, reliableplant.com, hipa.hu, dailynewshungary.com, kedali.eu, leclanche.com, evreporter.com, nexcharge.in, volkswagen-group.com, electrive.com, investmentmonitor.ai, kompass.com, debrecensun.hu, invest-in-thuringia.de, thelec.net, china-arn.com, areadevelopment.com, crainsdetroit.com, boi.gov.ph, stbgiga.com, business-sweden.com, koreaherald.com, mining.com, batteriesnews.com, cnevpost.com, businesswire.com, prnewswire.com, electrek.co, tataautocomp.com, energy-storage.news, autocarpro.in, greencarcongress.com, energy.gov, posventa.info. Fallback fDi Markets (FT) for S on R123 (SAFT Bangalore — $81.6M not publicly confirmed), R126 (Nexcharge Prantij — $77.6M not confirmed), R128 (Samsung SDI Noida — $70M not confirmed), R130 (Kedali Erfurt — $66M not confirmed), R131 (Samsung Xi'an — $63M not confirmed), R133 (OliPower New Clark City — P5-7B total JV vs $61M in dataset), R134 (Kedali Skellefteå — amount not confirmed), R138 (Gotion Fremont — $50M not confirmed), R139 (Gotion Pune — $50M not confirmed), R141 (JCI Guardamar — $47M expansion not confirmed). A-O reconciliations: R126 G 'Pune (Sabarkantha district)'→'Prantij' (evreporter.com confirms factory is at Prantij); R129 G Nyíregyháza→Debrecen (yicaiglobal + debrecensun.hu confirm Debrecen location); R130 I 2020→2022 (kedali.eu confirms spring 2022 completion; Pattern A); R133 G Manila→New Clark City (stbgiga.com/boi.gov.ph confirm Filinvest Innovation Park, New Clark City, Tarlac); R133 E Announced→Completed (BOI confirms factory opened September 2024); R133 I 2023→2024 (factory opened Sep 2024); R136 I 2023→2024 (CATL/Arun Plus production started Q4 2024; Pattern A); R139 E Announced→Completed (autocarpro.in confirms BESS plant inaugurated Nov 2023); R139 I 2019→2023 (inauguration 2023); R139 N None→1 (Tata AutoComp Systems is India-based local partner); R139 O 'Tata'→'Tata AutoComp Systems' (full JV partner name per tataautocomp.com). Flags: R124 W=[UNVERIFIED] (Sanyo Salem is solar silicon ingot/wafer plant, not battery — sector mismatch; sector column not reconcilable per rules); R125 S=[UNVERIFIED] (sources cite EUR 39.4M initial investment, dataset $78M — >30% discrepancy, Pattern B scope ambiguity with expansion announced separately); R127 S=[UNVERIFIED] (VW press release cites EUR 500M total Northvolt stake; $70M in dataset not directly confirmed); R136 S=[UNVERIFIED] (3.6B baht total JV = ~$104M; $52M likely CATL's ~50% share per Pattern B).

Sequential rows 142-161 (Umicore Cheonan 2008 through SAFT South Shields). Sources: umicore.kr, michiganbusiness.org, wzzm13.com, areadevelopment.com, detroitnews.com, globenewswire.com (Umicore 2012, A123 Czech, A123-SAIC JV, Boston-Power), greencarcongress.com (Umicore 2010), businesswire.com (A123 Czech), yuasa.de, gs-yuasa.com, hipa.hu, yuasa.co.uk, cnevpost.com, banpunext.co.th, chinadaily.com.cn, cninfo.com.cn, finance.sina.com.cn, news.91jinshu.com, news.utk.edu, tnresearchpark.org, ornl.gov, skoda-storyboard.com, etn.news, eepower.com, basf.com, reliableplant.com, borgwarner.com, electrive.com, gsyuasa-lp.com, leclanche.com, energy-storage.news, camelbatt.com, batteriesinternational.com, industryweek.com, embedded.com. Fallback fDi Markets (FT) for S on R142/R143/R146/R147/R149/R151/R152/R155/R156/R157 (amounts not confirmed in public sources); full fallback for R153 (Sanyo Suzhou 2003 — no Tier A/B source found for this 2003 investment after IR + local-language passes). A-O reconciliations: R144 M 1→0 / O 'Stellantis'→None (Auburn Hills expansion is Samsung SDI's own facility, not the StarPlus JV in Kokomo Indiana; confirmed by areadevelopment.com + detroitnews.com); R149 O None→'Banpu NEXT' (Banpu NEXT holds 40% of SVOLT Energy Thailand; confirmed by banpunext.co.th) / I 2023→2024 (mass production started Feb 2024 per chinadaily.com.cn; first pack Dec 2023 = commissioning, Pattern A); R157 M 1→0 / O 'CINEA'→None (CINEA is EU's European Climate, Infrastructure and Environment Executive Agency — EU grant body, not a JV equity partner; confirmed by energy-storage.news + batterytechassociation.org); R158 G 'Ann Arbor'→'Battle Creek' (the $20M factory is in Battle Creek MI per camelbatt.com; Ann Arbor is Camel's North American HQ/sales office, not the factory). Flags: R143 U=[UNVERIFIED] (wzzm13.com article is from Feb 2018, dataset Year Announced=2017 — possible year mismatch; no 2017-specific announcement found); R146 R/U/W=[UNVERIFIED] (GlobeNewsWire 2008 confirms Taiwan GP Batteries partnership but Taipei city not confirmed; source says 2008 announcement, dataset says 2009; activity mfg vs design/testing ambiguous); R150 R=[UNVERIFIED] (Chinese CNINFO filing confirms Chonburi Province; specific city within Chonburi unclear; dataset says Rayong which is a neighboring province — may need reconciliation on next audit); R152 W=[UNVERIFIED] (Skoda sources describe battery assembly/manufacturing, but dataset activity says Logistics — possible fDi Markets miscategorization).

Sequential rows 162-181 (Sanyo Dorog through CATL Munich). Sources: eib.org (Sanyo Hungary), ir.quantumscape.com (QuantumScape Kyoto), english.news.cn + technode.com (SAIC Chonburi), electrive.com (Toshiba Hansalpur), store-dot.com + prnewswire.com (StoreDot Irvine), ticotimes.net + elfinancierocr.com (Panasonic Alajuela), insideevs.com (VW Mlada Boleslav), en.gotion.com.cn + prnewswire.com + cnevpost.com (Gotion Thailand), saft.com (SAFT Zhuhai), wuling.id + cnevpost.com (SAIC-GM-Wuling Cikarang), en.calb-tech.com (CALB Rayong), bernama.com + energytrend.com (Gotion Malaysia), highstar.com + evlithium.com (Pret Thailand), oto.detik.com (SAIC+CATL Cikarang), gotion.asia + wuling.id (Gotion Jakarta), hopluccorp.vn (Desay Bac Giang), catl.com (CATL Munich). Fallback fDi Markets (FT) for S on most rows (amounts not independently confirmed), P on most rows (parent HQ), and full fallback for R167 (SHOTO Malaysia — no Tier A/B found) and R180 (CATL Bad Friedrichshall — no CATL presence in Bad Friedrichshall confirmed; CATL Germany is Munich+Erfurt). A-O reconciliations: R164 G 'Rayong'→'Chonburi' (SAIC battery plant is in Chonburi EEC zone per Xinhua Oct 2023; Rayong is adjacent province); R164 E 'Announced'→'Completed' (plant opened Oct 31 2023; technode.com+Xinhua); R166 G 'Los Angeles'→'Irvine' (StoreDot facility at UCI Research Park then 32 Discovery, Irvine CA; prnewswire.com Jan 2023); R171 I 2022→2023 (NV Gotion first battery pack off line Dec 7 2023; JV signed Dec 2022 = capex year = Pattern A; cnevpost.com); R173 E 'Announced'→'Completed' (SAIC-GM-Wuling Cikarang Magic Battery production line inaugurated Dec 31 2024; wuling.id); R174 G 'Bangkok'→'Rayong' (CALB Thailand Co. Ltd. established in Rayong EEC; en.calb-tech.com); R177 G 'Jakarta'→'Cikarang' (PT UABS Indonesia at SAIC International Industrial Park, Cikarang, Bekasi, West Java; oto.detik.com); R177 E 'Announced'→'Completed' (UABS operational Nov 5 2024; oto.detik.com); R179 G 'Ho Chi Minh City'→'Bac Giang' (Desay Battery Vina at Song Khe–Noi Hoang Industrial Park, Yen Dung District, Bac Giang Province; hopluccorp.vn). S=[UNVERIFIED] on R177 (source gives $6.3M vs dataset $5M — >20% discrepancy). Note: R169/R170 (VW Mlada Boleslav) are two distinct rows for the same 2018-2019 investment — one Manufacturing (battery PHEV assembly) and one Logistics; both confirmed at same site but logistics activity not independently confirmed by source.

Sequential rows 182-191 (BYD Manaus through CBAK+Kandi USA pack assembly). Sources: bydglobal.com + electrive.com (BYD Brazil — fully confirmed), borgwarner.com/acquires/sevcon + globenewswire.com (BorgWarner Gateshead), yicaiglobal.com (Zhongrui KNS South Korea), news.samsungsdi.com + theinvestor.vn (Samsung SDI Bac Ninh), bollore.com PDF press release (Bollore+Putailai Shanghai), sustainable-bus.com + borgwarner.com/technologies/battery-systems (BorgWarner Darmstadt mfg), northdata.com (BorgWarner Darmstadt R&D Technical Center GmbH), ir.cbak.com.cn + globenewswire.com (CBAK+Anker Malaysia; CBAK+Kandi USA x2). Fully sourced: R182 (BYD Manaus — bydglobal.com confirms China parent, Completed Sept 1 2020, Manaus PIM location, R$15M=$2.7M, LiFePO4 manufacturing). Flags: R184 R=[UNVERIFIED] (Seoul not specified; source says South Korea only); R184 S=[UNVERIFIED] (KRW5B≈$3.6M vs dataset $2.5M — ~44% discrepancy); R185 S=[UNVERIFIED] ($2M matches Samsung SDI global minimum tax allocation in Vietnam 2024, not confirmed capex — possible fDi data quality issue); R189 R=[UNVERIFIED] (Malaysia confirmed; KL city not specified in announcement); R190 R=[UNVERIFIED] (US confirmed; Atlanta not finalized — announcement states evaluating potential locations); R191 R=[UNVERIFIED] (same; Kandi-led 90% pack assembly JV, distinct from CBAK 90% cell mfg JV in R190). No A-O reconciliations in this batch.

[UNVERIFIED] applied: R192 R (Atlanta not confirmed — US facility location still under evaluation), R196 R (Princeton not confirmed — Samsung SDI/Solid Power announcement does not specify US city), R197 R (Houston not confirmed — SLAC's OKL Engineering partnership is in Cincinnati/Dayton area, not Houston), R202 R (Phylion Hungary city not confirmed — phylion.com homepage only), R203 R (Samsung SDI/Selex Vietnam city not confirmed). Data quality flag R204: Year Completed (2019) < Year Announced (2024) — likely fDi data entry error; not reconciled without authoritative source confirming Noida production start year.

Sequential rows 232-251 (JinkoSolar Bac Giang through China Glass Egypt TEDA). All Solar Manufacturing rows. A-O reconciliations: R235 H 2022→2021 (First Solar IR press release dated Sept 2021 confirms announcement year); R236 G None→'Ain Sokhna (SCZone)' (amwalalghad.com + agbi.com confirm SCZone Ain Sokhna); R237 I 2024→2023 (MIDA confirms operations commenced Oct 2023, ahead of 2024 schedule); R238 N 0→1 (Invenergy is US company; destination=USA — JV local partner); R251 L 300→310 (glassonweb.com + China.org.cn cite $310M for China Glass Egypt groundbreaking Nov 2024). Sequential rows 212-231 (Gotion India HAR0030 through NE Solar Cikarang). Mix of Battery (R212–R217) and Solar (R218–R231). A-O reconciliations: R213 N '0'→'1' (Johnson Controls is a US local JV partner; destination = USA); R221 N None→'1' (RELC and Vision Industries are both Saudi local partners per pvtime.org); R224 I '2025'→'2026' (United Solar production started Feb 2026 per pv-magazine.com; Pattern A — 2025 was projected year); R226 G 'Vietnam (Bac Giang)'→'Quang Ninh' (Song Khoai IP, Quang Yen, Quang Ninh confirmed by vir.com.vn — NOT Bac Giang); R226 E 'Announced'→'Cancelled' (JinkoSolar submitted termination notice Feb 26 2026; pv-magazine.com + technode.global confirm US tariff reason). [UNVERIFIED] applied: R218 S ($11.5B is announcement-stage pledge, not executed capex), R219 R (West Java confirmed, exact city/zone TBC), R219 S ($5B pledged, not confirmed executed capex), R220 S ($2.5B initial announcement revised to ~$2.75B per pv-tech; >10% discrepancy), R221 R (Saudi Arabia confirmed, specific city still TBD), R222 S ($1.8B not confirmed — total Sarawak investment ~MYR5.6B/~$1.3B), R223 S (RM5.2B = ~$1.62B; 2010 project never executed), R224 S ($1.6B dataset vs $1.35B per pv-tech; >15% discrepancy across sources), R225 S (RM7.2B ≈ $1.5B; Tier B only), R226 S ($1.5B initial, later revised to $294M before cancellation), R229 S ($940M not confirmed — $210M loan + ฿9B stated don't reconcile to $940M), R231 R (Indonesia confirmed, Cikarang not confirmed by available sources), R231 S ($800M not confirmed). Data quality flag R227: fDi Year Announced = 2024 but Maxeon original press release is Aug 10 2023; fDi may have recorded 2024 as the year of formal site commitment / Honeywell building lease. Not reconciled without confirmation.

Sequential rows 252-271 (Sharp/Enel 3Sun Catania through Canadian Solar Hai Phong). All Solar Manufacturing rows. Sources: enel.com (Sharp/Enel 3Sun Catania JV), pvtime.org (Flat Glass Group Central Java $290M), ecofinagency.com (CSG Holding Egypt $252M Ain Sokhna), investors.canadiansolar.com (Canadian Solar Mesquite TX $250M — fully confirmed Tier A), lessosolar.com (Lesso Semarang Jetenglan IP), investkorea.org (SoloPower Gwangju CTI Korea JV — InvestKorea Tier A govt portal), pv-tech.org (Almaden KIZAD Abu Dhabi $240M — fully confirmed), pv-magazine.com (HyET Solar India €200M; Sunrev Solar Ain Sokhna $200M — fully confirmed; FuturaSun Taizhou; Deye Malaysia $150M — fully confirmed), jinjing-glass.com (Jinjing Kedah Malaysia — Tier A company newsroom), pv-tech.org (JA Solar Egypt SCZONE $213M — fully confirmed), pvmagazine.com (Sunrev Solar Egypt), news.panasonic.com (Panasonic Jhajjar $200M), investor.firstsolar.com + gpms.biz (First Solar Frankfurt/Oder $200M), reneweconomy.com.au (Energy Puzzle Group Hambantota $190M — fully confirmed), en.vietnamplus.vn (JA Solar Bac Ninh suspended), investors.canadiansolar.com (Canadian Solar Hai Phong VSIP $150M). Full fallback fDi Markets (FT): R255 (Canadian Solar Ethiopia — Bloomberg coverage paywalled; parent company stated no committed decision as of Q2 2025); R269 (First Applied Material Thailand — no Tier A/B source found after company IR + trade press + local-language passes). A-O reconciliations: R253 G 'Batam Free Trade Zone (Riau Islands Province)'→'Central Java / Jawa Tengah (exact city TBC)' (pvtime.org confirms Jawa Tengah, not Batam — distinct geography); R254 G placeholder→'Ain Sokhna (SCZONE)' (ecofinagency.com confirms SCZONE/Ain Sokhna area); R261 G 'Kota Kinabalu'→'Kedah (Gulin High Tech Park)' (jinjing-glass.com newsroom confirms Jan 22 2022 inauguration at Gulin High Tech Park, Kedah — Kota Kinabalu KKIP is Kibing, a different company); R262 G 'Suez Ecnoomic Zone'→'Suez Canal Economic Zone (SCZONE)' (typo fix + expansion; pv-tech.org confirms); R263 E 'Announced'→'Commenced' (cornerstone ceremony June 2025 per pvmagazine.com). [UNVERIFIED] applied: R252 S (Enel cites €150M; dataset $292M — >30% discrepancy); R258 S (SoloPower: $125M initial vs $250M later phase — phased escalation per InvestKorea); R260 R (HyET Solar mentions Assam in trade press but location not finalized; company was seeking investors not directly committing); R261 S ($220M not confirmed in source); R265 S (First Solar Frankfurt source cites €161M; dataset $200M — slight discrepancy at 2011 exchange rates); R267 S (JA Solar Bac Ninh $189M in dataset vs $967M total investment per VietnamPlus — likely Phase 1 vs. total scope); R268 Q (pvmagazine Jul 2019 vs sources indicating 2020 opening — year discrepancy); R271 S ($150M dataset vs $70M IFC financing package — $70M is financing, not total capex). Data quality flags: R264 W=[UNVERIFIED] (Panasonic Jhajjar sources confirm AC/appliance/welding manufacturing, NOT solar — sector mismatch similar to R124 Sanyo Salem); R266 stale announcement (Energy Puzzle Group Sri Lanka signed Feb 2014, >12 years old, no construction evidence found).
